journal_red_flags: Omitting comprehensive ablation studies to justify architectural choices and newly proposed modules
Using non-standard, outdated, or insufficient evaluation metrics, datasets, and weak baselines instead of current state-of-the-art
Presenting purely theoretical algorithms or lengthy mathematical proofs without intuitive visual explanations or practical system implementations
Over-formalizing simple architectural modifications with unnecessary complex mathematical proofs or overloading the Introduction with dense equations
Ignoring computational cost, scalability, hardware efficiency, and processing speed, which are critical for video technology systems
Relying solely on qualitative visual results without rigorous quantitative validation or omitting failure cases and limitations
